memoryArtifacts.headline: Will of Thomas Foster
memoryArtifacts.description: Image of the Last Will and Testament of Thomas Foster of Wendover, England.  He mentions by name his wife Elizabeth, son Thomas, and daughters Cristian, Elizabeth, and Susann

memoryArtifacts.headline: Brief Biography
memoryArtifacts.description:
Biography

Susanna was born about 1616. She was baptized 14 Jul 1616 in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England. In Thomas Foster's will written 23 Feb 1618 Susanna was named as his daughter.[1]

Susanna F …

zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Seamer是Simon Seamer Jr和Jone Simson的女兒，於大約1584年出生在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾圖維爾。她於1606年11月10日與Thomas Foster在英國巴金哈姆爾圖維爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於從1640年到1659年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Seamer是Simon Seamer Jr和Jone Simson的女儿，于大约1584年出生在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾圖維爾。她于1606年11月10日与Thomas Foster在英国巴金哈姆爾圖維爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于从1640年到1659年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Seamer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Semer, Samar (Old English Sǣmǣr). Some of the following bearers may alternatively belong with (2) or (3). There may also have been confusion with Seymour .
2  English: locative name from either of two places called Seamer (NR Yorks) or either of two places called Semer (Norfolk, Suffolk).
3 English: occupational name from Middle English semer(e) ‘tailor’ (Old English sēamere). Compare Simister .

variants.name: Seymour
meanings: English (London): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), and possibly also from Saint-Maur-des-Bois (Manche) and Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine), named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Maur (see   Moore   3). The family of this name who have held the title Duke of Somerset since 1547 are said by some to have originated in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), while others claim they are from Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine). They rose to prominence under the Tudors, when Edward Seymour (died 1552) held a high position of office under Henry VIII, who was married to Edward's sister, Jane. The Norman name has been established in Ireland since at least the 17th century.

variants.name: Simister
meanings: 1 English: occupational name, variant of Sumpster (Middle English somester) with unrounding of /u/ to /ɪ/ before the nasal consonant. This is a post-medieval dialect feature of NW England and SW England, and perhaps also of the NW Midlands. Compare Simmers for Summers , Simnor for Sumner, Simpson for Sumsion .

2 English: occupational name from Middle English semester (Old English sēamestre) ‘sewer, tailor, sempstress’. The medieval name is mostly borne by women, and as such may not have become hereditary, but there are also a few men so named. Compare Seamer .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Seamer was born about 1584, in Turville, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Simon Seamer Jr and Jone Simson. She married Thomas Foster on 10 November 1606, in Turville, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died from 1640 to 1659, in Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Seamer est née environ 1584, à Turville, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de Simon Seamer Jr et Jone Simson. Elle a épousé Thomas Foster le 10 novembre 1606, à Turville, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée du 1640 au 1659, à Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Seamerは約1584年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアトゥルヴィルでSimon Seamer JrとJone Simsonの娘として生まれた。1606年11月10日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアトゥルヴィルでThomas Fosterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女はから1640年まで1659年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクドールチェスターで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Seamer(은)는 대략 1584년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 터르빌에서 Simon Seamer Jr(와)과 Jone Simson의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1606년 11월 10일 영국 벅잉함실르 터르빌에서 Thomas Foster(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1640년 부터 1659년 까지 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터에서 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas Foster em 10 de novembro de 1606, em Turville, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Seamer родилась около 1584 г. в местности турвилл, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. Её родителей звали Simon Seamer Jr и Jone Simson. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Foster 10 ноября 1606 г. в местности турвилл, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла с 1640 по 1659 в местности дорчестир, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.
